title: Melted Syrian Apples Flatbread
description: Crunchy iron-teppan flatbread studded with caramelized apples and a drizzle of honey, finished with a gentle flame for a smoky finish.
instructions: In a small bowl, dissolve the yeast in the warm water with a pinch of sugar and let it sit for 5 minutes until foamy.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, remaining sugar, and salt. Stir to mix.
Pour the activated yeast mixture and olive oil into the dry ingredients and knead until a smooth dough forms, then cover and let rise for 30 minutes.
While the dough rises, slice the apple into 5 mm thick rounds and toss them in honey, cinnamon, cardamom, and a pinch of sea salt.
Punch down the risen dough and divide it into four equal portions, rolling each into a flat round about 20 cm in diameter.
Preheat a cast-iron teppan over medium heat, then brush it lightly with olive oil.
Place a dough round on the hot teppan, and cook for 4 minutes until bubbles appear on the surface.
Flip the flatbread, then arrange the apple slices over the cooked side, drizzle additional honey, and place a small pat of butter on top.
Cover the teppan with a lid for 3 minutes to allow the butter to melt and the apples to soften.
Remove the flatbread, garnish with a sprinkle of sea salt, and serve hot.
